pattern: Every child except the root shows up exactly once as a child in the description list, so the one value that never appears in that slot is the root.
hints: Build every referenced value into a node once, keyed by value, then just wire left/right pointers between the already-built nodes as you scan the list.
Track which values ever appear as a child; whichever value only ever appears as a parent is the root.
One pass: a dict value -> node (creating nodes lazily on first sight), attach child to parent.left or parent.right per the flag, and a set of seen-as-child values to find the unique root afterward.

edge_inputs.description: two-level tree built out of order, root discoverable only after full scan
single description, minimal tree of two nodes
root value is larger than some child values, order in the list must not be assumed to be top-down
a node is parent to both a left and a right child listed non-adjacently

stress.description: randomly shaped tree of up to 3000 nodes, descriptions shuffled so the root is not first
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 3000))
    values = rng.sample(range(1, 100001), n)
    root = values[0]
    descriptions = []
    frontier = [root]
    remaining = values[1:]
    i = 0
    while i < len(remaining):
        parent = frontier[rng.randrange(len(frontier))]
        child = remaining[i]
        i += 1
        is_left = 1 if rng.random() < 0.5 else 0
        # avoid giving a parent two children on the same side
        used_sides = {d[2] for d in descriptions if d[0] == parent}
        if is_left in used_sides:
            is_left = 1 - is_left
            if is_left in used_sides:
                continue
        descriptions.append([parent, child, is_left])
        frontier.append(child)
    rng.shuffle(descriptions)
    return [descriptions]
stress.note: each parent gets at most one left and one right child by construction; shuffled so root discovery needs a full scan
